text: I arrive as open-source developer deciding whether to install the skill. My past: I try agent plugins carefully because bad tools clutter my local setup.
Today I want to: Find the install command, supported harnesses, docs, and license clarity.
My life goal is: Use tools I can inspect and extend without becoming dependent on opaque SaaS.
I have 100 life-years. Each meaningful action costs 1 month(s); each committed dollar costs 1 year(s).

label: arrival
title: Betabots - Synthetic beta users with real product evidence

text: I see "Betabots - Synthetic beta users with real product evidence".
I think: I see the promise in the title, but I do not yet see the install path, docs, harness support, or license.
My reaction: My first reaction is guarded interest; the evidence claim matters to me, but the page has not earned trust yet.
Idea: show install command, supported harnesses, documentation link, and license near the first screen.
Life-cost decision: One more action is worth 1 month if it reveals whether this supports my goal of using inspectable, extensible tools.
Truth assessment: My honest judgment is that I am interested but unconvinced, with high confidence because my decision needs concrete setup and inspection details.
